extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD won the ITT by 1:38 over Pogačar using a TT bike all the way up the final 2.5km 9.4% climb, extending GC lead to 1:48
tactical_patterns: Vingegaard used TT bike through entire 9.4% final climb while most switched to road bike, requiring exceptional power/discipline.
Pogačar caught his 2-minute man Rodriguez in final 300m, using him as draft/pacing reference to extract marginal gains.
Early corner crashes (3 riders including 2 DSM) suggest recon failure or overconfidence on technical descent section.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: gained 52 seconds on Pogačar between intermediate checks despite starting 2 minutes behind, unprecedented TT dominance
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: second-best time by over a minute but lost 1:28 in GC, suggests physical limits reached mid-tour
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: won sprinters' competition at 36:07, significantly outperformed expectations on climbing TT
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: moved to third overall, outperformed twin Simon and Rodriguez on climbing TT discipline
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Rémi: neutral: early leader but faded on final climb, confirms road bike switch cost time versus Vingegaard's approach
